tasks.description: Add the shared interaction policy model and provider/context wiring: create the `mobile-interaction-model` helper; extend `useTimelineState.ts`, `useTimelineState.types.ts`, `useTimelineState.contexts.ts`, `TimelineEditorContext.tsx`, and `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`/`VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` so `deviceClass`, per-event `inputModality`, `interactionMode`, `gestureOwner`, `precisionEnabled`, `contextTarget`, and `inspectorTarget` flow through `useTimelineEditorData()` / `useTimelineEditorOps()` without bypassing the provider, while keeping `interactionStateRef` as the only save/poll gate.

Make shell and inspector affordances policy-driven in `VideoEditorShell.tsx`, `PropertiesPanel.tsx`, and `ClipPanel.tsx`: add the phone mode bar (`Browse`, `Select`, `Move`, `Trim`, `Precision`), make tablet properties access selection-aware and faster than the current preview/properties toggle alone, and expose inspector-first alternatives for trim, move, move-to-track, split, delete, mute, and clip actions so common phone edits never require fragile direct manipulation.

Implement modality-aware timeline arbitration in `TimeRuler.tsx`, `TimelineEditor.tsx`, `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, `useMarqueeSelect.ts`, and `useClipDrag.ts`: resolve gesture ownership from device class plus `PointerEvent.pointerType`, keep the ruler as the dedicated scrub zone, make empty-space touch drag pan by default, gate `touch-action`/`preventDefault`/pointer capture until ownership is granted, enlarge touch trim hit zones, and preserve grouped drag plus deferred-save behavior by continuing to set `interactionStateRef.current.drag/resize` only when editing actually starts.

Apply the selection, move, context, and precision contract in `useMultiSelect.ts`, `useTimelineSelection.ts`, `useClipDrag.ts`, `ClipAction.tsx`, `TimelineCanvas.tsx`, `ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx`, and `useKeyboardShortcuts.ts`: implement the phone-vs-iPad selection and move rules, visible overflow alternatives to long-press, frame-based precision nudges, and preserved desktop/hardware-keyboard semantics; fold in the open shot-group touch gap so navigation, unpin/delete, and final-video switching are reachable without hover or `onContextMenu`-only affordances.

Bring preview overlay editing onto the same interaction contract in `OverlayEditor.tsx`, `PreviewPanel.tsx`, and `VideoEditorShell.tsx`: switch to pointer-driven ownership, keep phone overlay transforms inspector-first, prevent preview interactions from stealing touches intended for the inspector or timeline mode bar, and apply shared accessibility rules across shell, timeline, and preview including 44x44 touch targets, no hover-only actions, screen-reader labels for interaction state, and reduced-motion-safe transitions.

Extend the existing automated coverage for the new contract: add pure decision-table assertions in an existing nearby suite, expand `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`, `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, `useClipDrag.test.tsx`, `ClipAction.test.tsx`, `PreviewPersistence.test.tsx`, and `useTimelinePersistence.test.tsx` to cover provider publication, touch pan-vs-edit ownership, trim-handle gating, long-press move arming, tablet touch-vs-mouse splits, shot-group touch entry points, and persistence/layout regressions without introducing a second save path.

Run the full verification loop as the final task: execute the most relevant existing editor test suites for provider, shell, timeline canvas, clip action, clip drag, preview persistence, and timeline persistence coverage; write a short temporary repro script that exercises device-class plus `pointerType` arbitration and confirms iPad touch behavior does not activate for iPad mouse/trackpad, run it, delete it, then complete manual smoke checks on iPhone Safari, iPad Safari touch, and iPad Safari with hardware keyboard/trackpad. If anything fails, fix the code and rerun until green.

watch_items: Do not fork a second mobile timeline; extend the existing editor shell, timeline, preview, and provider stack.
Thread the interaction policy through `VideoEditorProvider` and the split editor contexts; downstream consumers should not bypass the provider by reading `useTimelineState()` directly.
Resolve behavior from baseline device class plus per-event `PointerEvent.pointerType`; iPad touch rules must never leak onto iPad mouse/trackpad or pen interactions.
`interactionStateRef` remains the sole save/poll deferral gate for drag and resize; do not add a second persistence suppression path.
Phone phase 1 stays inspector-first for complex edits, overlay transforms, and track reassignment; direct manipulation is additive, not mandatory.
Empty-space touch drag should pan by default; marquee or edit gestures only win when the current mode and gesture owner explicitly allow them.
Trim starts only from explicit handles or `Trim` mode, with touch-expanded hit zones activated only after ownership is granted.
Desktop and hardware-keyboard flows must remain intact, including current pointer semantics and `useKeyboardShortcuts()` behavior on iPad with keyboard/trackpad.
Address open FLAG-003 during execution: shot-group navigation, unpin/delete, and final-video switching need visible, non-hover, non-`onContextMenu` touch entry points and 44x44 targets.
Preview interactions must not steal touches intended for the inspector sheet, timeline mode bar, or timeline canvas.
Apply shared accessibility rules across shell, timeline, and preview: no hover-only affordances, screen-reader state labels, and reduced-motion-safe transitions.

Use existing phone/tablet buckets from `src/shared/hooks/mobile/index.ts` as the device-class signal; do not introduce new responsive breakpoint logic unless implementation proves the current buckets insufficient.
Manual smoke validation must cover iPhone Safari, iPad Safari touch, and iPad Safari with hardware keyboard/trackpad on the same build.

sense_checks.question: Can shell, timeline, preview, and properties consumers read and update the new interaction policy through `useTimelineEditorData()` / `useTimelineEditorOps()` without calling `useTimelineState()` directly, and does `interactionStateRef` remain the only persistence gate?
On phone and tablet layouts, are explicit mode controls and inspector fallbacks visible enough that common edits do not require fragile direct touch manipulation?
Do ruler scrub, empty-canvas pan or marquee, clip drag, and trim claim ownership correctly by `pointerType` and device class, with iPad mouse or trackpad still following desktop behavior?

Do phone and iPad selection, move, context, shot-group, precision, and keyboard rules match the contract, with visible non-hover touch entry points for actions previously gated by right-click or hover?
Does preview overlay editing follow the same interaction contract and accessibility rules without stealing gestures from the mode bar, inspector, or timeline?

Do the updated automated tests cover provider wiring, decision-table routing, timeline arbitration, touch trim gating, shot-group touch actions, and deferred-save regressions using the existing test infrastructure?
Have the targeted suites, temporary repro script, and manual Safari device checks all passed after reruns, with the repro script removed from the repo?

meta_commentary: Execute in the approved order: land the policy and context plumbing first, then shell and inspector affordances, then timeline gesture ownership, then higher-risk selection or move or trim behavior, and only after that convert preview overlay interactions. Keep the implementation centered on one shared interaction policy and one persistence gate; if a shortcut seems to require a mobile-only codepath or a second save or sync guard, stop and reroute through the existing provider/context surface and `interactionStateRef`. The one intentional refinement beyond the approved steps is to absorb FLAG-003 into the context-action work so touch users gain reliable shot-group actions while the gesture contract is being changed. Because this finalize brief forbids new standalone test files, satisfy the plan’s pure-policy coverage by extending existing nearby suites unless the repository already has an accepted colocated pattern that does not increase test-surface sprawl.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Add provider-backed interaction policy and modality resolver through the shared editor contexts
Make shell and inspector affordances policy-driven for phone and tablet editing
Route scrub, pan, marquee, drag, and trim by device class plus input modality
Define selection, move, context, precision, and keyboard behavior across touch and desktop inputs
Bring preview overlay editing and accessibility onto the same interaction contract
Validate the policy with automated coverage, a temporary repro harness, and manual device checks

validation.completeness_notes: All six approved plan steps are mapped. Step 6’s testing intent is preserved, but the file-placement detail is adapted to the finalize requirement that forbids introducing new standalone test files; pure-policy assertions should be folded into existing suites. T4 intentionally absorbs the still-open shot-group touch concern from FLAG-003 so that critique feedback remains visible during execution rather than being deferred out of the interaction pass.
